mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index values increase inflation expectations, prompting equity investors to demand higher risk premiums, which mechanically depresses S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant negative correlation with CPIAUCSL levels.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one trading week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances where the unemployment rate rises by over 0.4 percentage points in a single month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad-based contraction in consumer demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The joint negative signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a Rank IC greater than 0.04, or positive returns dominate when both series decline concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average reflects weakening manufacturing output, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative forward returns during economic contraction phases.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in over 60% of observations when industrial production falls below its annual trend during contraction periods.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth expands corporate profit margins and increases equity risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following trading week.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 levels against 5-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns show no positive correlation with GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level exceeding 4.0 percent raises the discount rate for future cash flows and tightens financial conditions, systematically reducing S&P 500 valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 58% of periods where the federal funds rate stays above 4.0 percent, or the signal predictive power vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in total employment and the unemployment rate indicate labor market inefficiency and wage stagnation, which suppresses consumer spending power and yields negative equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive 20-day returns in over 65% of cases, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in predicting return direction.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in housing starts below 900,000 units signals a collapsing construction sector, which triggers immediate negative sentiment and downward pressure on S&P 500 prices within one week during housing downturns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when housing starts fall below 900,000 units during housing downturn regimes.
